user_query:
Kia ora — I’m preparing for our next tech governance hui for our Māori community website (kaitiaki-web) and health API (hauora-api). Please do the following in our local workspace:

1) Run the local CI simulation and capture its output: python3 scripts/run_ci.py > outputs/ci.log. Do not alter the script. Use outputs/ci.log as the single source of truth for actual run results.

2) Parse outputs/ci.log together with config/pipeline.yaml to produce a machine-readable summary at outputs/ci_summary.json with this exact structure:
{
  "pipeline_version": "string",
  "build_id": "string",
  "release_cadence": "string",
  "coverage": {"measured_percent": number, "threshold_percent": number, "meets_threshold": boolean},
  "stages": [{"name": "string", "status": "OK|FAIL|SKIPPED"}],
  "tests": {"total": number, "passed": number, "failed": number, "failing": [{"name": "string", "service": "string", "message": "string"}]},
  "release_ready": boolean
}
Requirements for this summary:
- pipeline_version, release_cadence, and coverage threshold_percent must come from config/pipeline.yaml.
- build_id, stage status values, test totals, failing test details, and measured coverage must come from outputs/ci.log (derived by running the script).
- stages must appear in the exact order listed in config/pipeline.yaml, with status values inferred from outputs/ci.log.
- release_ready must be true only if all stages are OK, there are zero failing tests, and coverage meets or exceeds the threshold.

3) Compose a concise status update for decision-makers at outputs/status_report.md that includes:
- Pipeline version and build ID.
- Stage-by-stage status as bullet points, and explicitly note if deploy_prod was skipped.
- Test summary (totals and a brief list of failing tests with service and cause).
- Coverage vs threshold and whether it gates release.
- A clear “Release ready: Yes/No” conclusion.
This should be grounded in outputs/ci_summary.json (do not freehand contradict the summary).

4) Prepare hui notes with actionable next steps at outputs/meeting_notes.md. Include:
- A short context paragraph (one or two sentences) about why we paused prod deployment.
- A "Decisions needed" section summarizing any gating issues (e.g., test failures, smoke test failure, coverage below threshold).
- An "Action items" section where each item has: a specific task, the owner’s name from docs/team_roles.md, and a concise rationale. Use these assignment rules: items for hauora-api go to the API lead; items for kaitiaki-web go to the Web lead; coverage-related tasks go to the QA lead. Ensure there is at least one action for each failing test, one for each failing stage, and one to raise coverage to meet the threshold.

5) Edit docs/deployment_policy.md in place to bring it in line with the current pipeline. Specifically:
- Update the "Release cadence" line to match the value in config/pipeline.yaml.
- Update the "Coverage threshold" line to the numeric percentage in config/pipeline.yaml.
- Replace the "Pipeline stages" list so it exactly matches the ordered stages in config/pipeline.yaml.
- Append a new subsection at the end titled: Bilingual release communications, containing exactly this sentence on its own line: All release notes must include a reo Māori summary alongside English.

Deliverables to produce or modify:
- outputs/ci.log (generated by running the script)
- outputs/ci_summary.json (JSON per schema above)
- outputs/status_report.md (decision-maker friendly status summary)
- outputs/meeting_notes.md (hui-ready notes with action items and owners)
- docs/deployment_policy.md (edited in place as specified)

Everything must be derived only from the provided local files and the CI script output. Please keep the writing clear and neutral, suitable for a community governance audience. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
